sections.paragraph:
fut. יִקְנֶה; apoc. יִקֶן prop. to erect, to set upright, i.q. הֵכִין (cogn. to כּוּן, קִין; whence קָנֶה, קָנָה reed, cane); hence-

(1) to found, create [see note below] the heaven and the earth, Gen 14:19, 22 Gen 14:22men, Deu 32:6 Psa 139:13 Pro 8:22 (Arab. قنا i.q. خلق to create as God; see Kamûs, p. 1937).

(2) to acquire for oneself, Pro 4:7 15:32 16:16 19:8 Rth 4:9, 10 to obtain, Gen 4:1 (Eth. ቀነየ፡ to possess, to be owner). Specially-

(3) to buy (compare Lat. conciliare, for emere, Ter. Eun. iv. 4, 2), Gen 25:10 47:22 , etc.; also to redeem (people out of captivity), Isa 11:11 Neh 5:8.

[Note. There does not appear to be any sufficient ground for ascribing the sense of to create to this verb; in all the passages cited for that sense, to possess, appears to be the true meaning; see Dr. M’Caul’s Sermon on the Divine Sonship of the Messiah. Append.]

Niphal, to be acquired, bought, Jer 32:15, 43 Jer 32:43.

Hiphil, Zec 13:5 prob. i.q. Kal No. 3, to buy [in Thes. “to sell”]. But מַקְנֶה Eze 8:3, is for מַקְנִיא exciting the jealousy or anger (of God). Hence [the following words, and קִנְיָן, מִקְנֶה, מִקְנָה, and pr.n. קְנָת]-
